mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, enabling efficient deployment of large language models across various hardware accelerators. It offers intelligent scheduling, disaggregated serving, and workload autoscaling to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate resources to support high-performance workloads.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the llm-d command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI and makes it executable.
commands: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
Deploy llm-d using Helm to manage the application lifecycle.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install the llm-d chart in a dedicated namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> This example configures autoscaling for the llm-d deployment.
> This example sets up monitoring for llm-d.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
Remove the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data related to llm-d.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> This command removes the llm-d release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n llm-d' and adjust configurations as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the llm-d deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up the nodes or adjust resource requests in the deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly exposed or the application is not running.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure the application pods are running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators, improved scheduling algorithms, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.

description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
